name: react-component
description:
Use when creating UI components in React - functional components, hooks, custom hooks, or component composition patterns.
NOT when backend logic, API routes, or non-React frameworks are involved.
Triggers: "create component", "build widget", "KPI card", "form", "modal", "custom hook", "useContext", "useState", "useEffect".
qualityReason: Clear trigger and focused component guidance
capabilities: Create functional React components with TypeScript props interfaces
Build custom hooks with proper state management and cleanup
Implement component composition patterns using children and render props
Apply React.memo, useCallback, and useMemo for performance optimization
Handle loading states, error states, and accessibility requirements
Generate unit tests and Storybook stories for components
author: Awais68
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance and TypeScript code examples. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or environment variable access. All static analyzer findings are false positives: backticks in TypeScript code blocks were misidentified as shell execution, and metadata fields were flagged as file access. This is a legitimate React development guidance skill.
valueStatement: Create React functional components and hooks with TypeScript. This skill provides patterns for building accessible, performant, and maintainable UI components following modern React best practices.
limitations: Does not generate backend API routes or server-side code
Does not configure build tools or bundlers
Does not install dependencies or modify package.json
Focused on React only; other frameworks not covered

useCases.title: Build UI widgets and KPI cards
useCases.description: Create reusable KPI cards, data tables, and dashboard widgets for enterprise applications

useCases.title: Extract reusable custom hooks
useCases.description: Refactor component logic into custom hooks with proper typing and error handling

useCases.title: Build form components with validation
useCases.description: Create form components with TypeScript, validation, and accessible error states

promptTemplates.title: Create simple component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a React functional component called StudentKPICard that displays a student's name and attendance percentage. Use TypeScript and include a loading state.

promptTemplates.title: Build custom hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a custom hook called useAttendance that fetches student attendance data. Include loading state, error handling, and a refetch function.

promptTemplates.title: Component with composition
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a reusable Card component using composition patterns. It should accept a title, children content, and optional footer. Use TypeScript.

promptTemplates.title: Form with validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a StudentForm component using React Hook Form. Include name and email fields with required validation and error display.

bestPractices: Always use functional components with explicit TypeScript props interfaces
Include cleanup functions in useEffect to prevent memory leaks
Apply React.memo and useCallback for components receiving same props frequently
Ensure accessibility with proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support
antiPatterns: Avoid using class components; use functional components with hooks instead
Do not use any types; always define explicit TypeScript interfaces
Avoid hardcoded text; prepare components for internationalization
Do not skip error handling or loading states for async operations

faq.question: What React version does this skill support?
faq.answer: Supports React 18 and 19+ with modern hooks patterns and latest TypeScript best practices.

faq.question: Does this skill create test files?
faq.answer: Yes, it generates Jest/Vitest test files and Storybook stories for each component.

faq.question: Can I use this with Next.js?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns work with Next.js App Router and Pages Router components.

faq.question: Is data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No. This is a pure prompt skill with no data storage, transmission, or external calls.

faq.question: Does this work with shadcn/ui components?
faq.answer: Yes, it integrates shadcn/ui patterns and uses their component conventions.

faq.question: How does this compare to generic code generation?
faq.answer: Provides React-specific patterns, TypeScript typing, accessibility, and testing built-in.
